entries.definition:
يَحْمُوم yaḥmūm [n.] thick, black smoke; hot fire; canopy of hot
fire (56:43) وَظِلٍّ مِّن يَحْمُومٍ and a shadow of black smoke.
entries.definition_english:
يَحْمُوم yaḥmūm [n.] thick, black smoke; hot fire; canopy of hot
fire (56:43) وَظِلٍّ مِّن يَحْمُومٍ and a shadow of black smoke.

entries.definition:
ḥ–m–w/y sanctuary, protection, to protect, to rebel, to avoid;
in-laws; to heat up, to become angry, hot-headedness, zeal,
fervour, impetuosity. Of this root, four forms occur six times in
the Qurʾan: يُحْمَى yuḥmā once; حَامِ ḥāmin once; حَامِيَة ḥāmiyatun
twice and حَمِيَّة ḥamiyyatun twice.

يُحْمَى yuḥmā [imperf. of pass. v. حَمِيَ ḥumiya, with prep. عَلَى] to
be heated, to be burnt, to be brought to a hot condition (9:35) يَوْمَ
يُحْمَى عَلَيْهَا فِي نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ فَتُكْوَى بِهَا جِبَاهُهُمْ وَجُنُوبُهُمْ وَظُهُورُهُمْ on the day when
they will be heated in the fire of Hell, and their foreheads, sides
and backs will be branded by them.

حَامِ ḥāmi [nominalised act. part.] an old stud-camel left by
pagan Arabs to spend the rest of its life in peace and tranquillity
(5:103) مَا جَعَلَ اللَّهُ مِن بَحِيرَةٍ وَلَا سَائِبَةٍ وَلَا وَصِيلَةٍ وَلَا حَامٍ وَلَكِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا
يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى اللَّهِ الْكَذِبَ God did not institute the dedication of such as
baḥiratun, saʾibatun, waṣīlatun (q.v.) or ḥām [to idols]; but the
disbelievers invent lies about God.

حَامِيَة ḥāmiyatun [act. part. fem.] burning hot, blazing (88:4)
تَصْلَى نَارًا حَامِيَةً scorched by a blazing fire.

حَمِيَّة ḥamiyyatun [v. n./n.] fiery fervour, high spirits, eagerness,
passion, rashness, zeal, impetuousness (48:26) إِذْ جَعَلَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فِي
قُلُوبِهِمُ الْحَمِيَّةَ حَمِيَّةَ الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ while the disbelievers had excited fervour in
their hearts—the fervour of ignorance.
